title: Understanding SQL Joins
headline: Write Complex SQL Joins With Confidence in Just 2 Sessions
subheadline: Struggling with joins in real-world scenarios? This hands-on workshop helps you actually build them.
painPoints: You understand joins in theory but can't apply them in real projects
You get confused when multiple tables are involved
You rely on Google or Stack Overflow for basic queries
You avoid writing joins and ask someone else to do it
transformation: Write multi-table joins confidently from scratch
Understand when to use INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL, CROSS, and SELF joins
Debug broken queries faster by reading execution flow
Handle real-world data relationships without hesitation
whatYouWillDo: Solve practical SQL challenges using realistic datasets
Build multi-table queries step-by-step with live guidance
Work through real-world scenarios involving orders, customers, and products
Practice debugging incorrect joins and fixing common mistakes
prerequisites: You should have basic SQL experience - you can write SELECT, WHERE, and simple GROUP BY queries. No advanced knowledge is needed. You'll need access to SQL Server Management Studio or Azure Data Studio.
whyDifferent: Live query building with real datasets - not slides
Real-world scenarios instead of textbook theory
Small group size so you can ask questions and get direct feedback
The databases and sandbox remains available to keep practicing after the workshop
Badge is assigned to your LinkedIn profile to showcase your new skills to employers

instructor.name: Mehfooz Kausmally
instructor.description: A seasoned software professional from Mauritius with over a decade of experience in the IT industry. After a successful career as a developer in multinational companies working extensively with complex databases and backend systems, Mehfooz transitioned into full-time professional coaching. He has already guided over 270 engineers across different technologies and levels  bringing real-world expertise and a practical, hands-on approach into every session.
